name: RABBIT-FILE-BACKLOG-14
title: Wave-2 deferred: spec note on worst-case push-attempt budget under contention
description:
Deferred from wave-1 housekeeping (info-only; trivial size, kept out of BACKLOG-13 cycle).

Worst-case per filing: 16 (counter push retries) + 16 (item push retries) + 16 (sha-backfill push retries) = 48 push attempts under sustained contention, each preceded by fetch+reset, bounded by jittered backoff (max ~2.1s per retry per spec). On a true outage this can stall ~30s per script invocation.

This is NOT a code change — current retry budget (16 per push) is appropriate and the jittered backoff prevents thundering herd. It's an observability/documentation note so operators and future maintainers understand the worst-case timing before optimizing.

Resolution: add a one-paragraph note to spec.md (under the retry invariant or in a new 'Operational characteristics' section) documenting:
- Per-filing worst-case: 48 push attempts (3 pushes × 16 retries each)
- Per-retry budget: jittered backoff max ~2.1s
- Worst-case wall time: ~30s per script invocation under sustained outage
- This is acceptable per current usage patterns; revisit if contention measurements show real impact.

Acceptance: spec.md carries the operational-characteristics note; no code changes; no test changes required.
